claims.text: The EU Commission launched a formal investigation into Chinese wind turbine manufacturer Goldwind under the Foreign Subsidies Regulation, signalling active state-backed competitive pressure on NCE-based manufacturers.

Sinovel Wind Group was convicted by a US federal court for theft of wind turbine control software from AMSC, establishing a confirmed precedent of Chinese state-linked actors targeting Western wind OT intellectual property through insider recruitment.

The EU Industrial Accelerator Act (2026) formally designates wind energy as a strategic sector, elevating it as a high-value intelligence collection target for adversarial states seeking technological parity with European manufacturers.

The EU Institute for Security Studies (March 2026) identified competition over clean technology supply chains and industrial capacity as a central axis of US-China geopolitical rivalry directly impacting European energy sector security.

Volt Typhoon (Chinese state-sponsored actor) has been assessed by CISA and Five Eyes partners as pre-positioning persistent access within critical infrastructure OT networks across Western nations, with specific focus on staging disruption capability in energy sector environments.

Sandworm (Russian GRU-linked actor) has conducted confirmed destructive attacks against European OT and energy infrastructure, with the 2015 and 2016 Ukrainian power grid incidents serving as the documented operational precedent now assessed by CISA and Five Eyes as applicable to NCE energy operators.

A coordinated attack on 22 Danish energy companies in 2023 represents the geographically closest confirmed OT-targeting incident to NCE wind manufacturing operations, demonstrating that state-directed campaigns against the European energy sector have already materialised at scale.

No confirmed intrusion, data exfiltration, or ransomware staging incident specifically attributable to NCE wind manufacturing networks or their named supply chain partners is present in signals recovered during the current collection window.

CER Directive transposition delays have left NCE wind turbine manufacturers without formal critical entity designation, removing state-level protective obligations that would otherwise apply to their manufacturing and engineering infrastructure under EU law.

Volt Typhoon's documented low-and-slow pre-positioning methodology is specifically designed to evade detection — the absence of a confirmed breach of NCE manufacturing systems in open-source collection is consistent with, not contradictory to, an active but undetected intrusion campaign at this level of target value.
